agentskills/agentskills#15.body:
I highly appreciate that skills is becoming a standard that we can use across many different agent harnesses. 

To make it even smoother, I believe that agreeing on a standard name for the agent skills folder would benefit everyone. This would allow different harnesses to have a default folder to find skills without requiring each project to put them in a different folder and create symlinks that match the specific folder of each harness.

Example: Claude is looking at `.claude/skills/` and Github Copilot is looking at `.github/skills/`. 

Edit: now we have Codex also looking at its own folder `.codex/skills/`

My suggestion for the name would be `.agents/skills/`, but I don't really care much about the name as long as it's agreed upon and standardized. It could even be just `.skills/`.

## Adoption

Please help to keep the lists below up to date. 😄 

### Ready

* Amp: https://ampcode.com/manual#agent-skills
* Augment Code (Auggie): https://docs.augmentcode.com/cli/skills#skill-file-structure
* Goose: https://block.github.io/goose/docs/guides/context-engineering/using-skills/#skill-locations
* Codex: https://github.com/openai/codex/pull/10317 and https://github.com/openai/codex/pull/10437
* VS Code: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/292414
* VTCode: https://github.com/vinhnx/VTCode/issues/600
* Pi: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/1211 (configurable)
* Mistral Vibe: https://github.com/mistralai/mistral-vibe/blob/ec7f3b25eaf47c447f4005561ae2ff32545d3dff/CHANGELOG.md?plain=1#L14
* OpenCode: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/pull/11842
* OpenClaw: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9966
* Gemini CLI: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/pull/18151
* Craft Agents: https://github.com/lukilabs/craft-agents-oss/issues/171
* RooCode: https://github.com/RooCodeInc/Roo-Code/issues/11368 (implemented and released but not documented yet)
* Cursor: https://x.com/leerob/status/2024141055143383184 (released)
* Windsurf: released on version 1.9552.21 (2026-02-12)
* Google Antigravity: https://antigravity.google/docs/skills
* Gwen Code: implemented on https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code/issues/2155

### Pending

* Claude Code: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/16345
* KiloCode: https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/discussions/5783
* Github: https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills/issues/15#issuecomment-3837334040 (tracking work internally, will give an update when done)

## Not adopting yet

There are too many tools to list here. Anyone not mention in "ready" or "pending" is considered not adopting yet.
agentskills/agentskills#15.title: Proposal: include a standard folder where agent skills should be

microsoft/vscode#103451.body:
VS Code, as any Windows application, should respect system-wide settings of metered connections.
It's bad experience for people who are on the go and connected via mobile internet.

Proposed behavior:
1. VS Code doesn't attempt to update itself or extensions when on metered connection.
2. When user manually clicks to update, it should display popup dialog with confirmation to start update.
3. (optionally) Expose API to extensions.

Related:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/75195
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/8540
microsoft/vscode#103451.title: Support metered connections

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: v1.71.0 - OS Version: macOS v12.5.1 ## NOTE It's new issue for wrongly closed #159071 ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Use multi-root workspace with Javascript Node.js & React.js projects 2. Try to use IntelliSense and auto-complete ### Updated steps - Download https://github.com/dalisoft/vscode-workspace-bug/archive/refs/heads/master.zip **DO NOT CLONE, DOWNLOAD ZIP FILE FOR BETTER REPRODUCE** - Run `./git-init.sh` (Linux & macOS) for imitiate `Git` powered repo - Open `workspace.code-workspace` with VSCode - Install dependencies for all workspaces - Try to work with IntelliSense on both `backend` and `frontend` folder, you'll see the bug ### Settings

Workspace settings

 ```json { "folders": [ { "path": "api-shared" }, { "path": "ui-shared" }, { "path": "backend" }, { "path": "frontend" }, ], "settings": { "git.autofetchPeriod": 60, "task.allowAutomaticTasks": "on", "typescript.tsserver.useSyntaxServer": "always", "typescript.tsserver.experimental.enableProjectDiagnostics": true, "typescript.tsserver.maxTsServerMemory": 5120, "typescript.tsserver.log": "verbose", "cSpell.enabled": false, "eslint.workingDirectories": [ "./api-shared", "./ui-shared", "./backend", "./frontend", ], } } ```
### [Global settings](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/dalisoft/376c3dfc48eae5a95d15cfc87639f6cf/raw/dc23b4c1f50db7d6127d2024ad8f02b4143d9bfd/settings.json) ## Actual https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3511344/187973344-430f76e9-e778-4489-9044-314c99d24dc3.mp4 ## Excepted https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3511344/187976968-f7ab2f6d-9df8-4d89-b177-ce35a02194cc.mp4 ## How to switch between Actual & Excepted? Just open `backend` folder from VSCode ## Interesting facts - On my daily using (6-8 folders with macOS Native tabs) using Workspaces feature reduces my memory usage up-to 4-5 times (from 14GB to ~3GB) while increasing CPU usage by 3-x (so coolers forgets about silent) - On workspaces (6-8 folders) using launch tasks uses CPU very aggressively so CPU coolers keeps at max almost always - On workspaces easier to work and opening terminals, so it increases productivity up-to 20% and it's huge if calculate per year

microsoft/vscode#182212.body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: TBD - VS Code Version: ``` Version: 1.78.1 Commit: 6a995c4f4cc2ced6e3237749973982e751cb0bf9 Date: 2023-05-04T10:01:22.349Z (1 wk ago) Electron: 22.5.1 Chromium: 108.0.5359.215 Node.js: 16.17.1 V8: 10.8.168.25-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 22.4.0 Sandboxed: No ``` - OS Version: macOS 13.3.1 (a) (22E772610a) Steps to Reproduce: I've had this happen twice now, once today and last time was on May 5th. I try to connect to my remote session and I get this: I try to jump into my macOS terminal, which I typically always have open anyway and run some commands, ssh works fine of course, perplexed... I wound up trying to open a new tab with a new terminal and it fails... After much digging I find this: ``` % lsof | grep /dev/ptmx | grep Code | wc -l 510 ``` vscode is using all of my available TTY fd's. This happened in the course of 2 days (I rebooted the machine 2 days ago). It is worth noting right now I have 3 vscode windows open, all disconnected from the remote. Closing all vscode windows (but with the vscode process still running as happens on macOS until you cmd+q it) does not change this number. Actually quitting vscode gives me all my tty fd's back. (let's assume from now on when I say fd I'm referring to /dev/ptmx fd's) On a brand new vscode instance (no remote connection or anything), just a window open there are 0 open fd's from vscode. Opening one terminal in vscode there are now 2 fd's opened. After closing that terminal there is 1 fd open. Opening a new terminal there are 3 fd's open. Closing that terminal there are 2 fd's open now. Open a new terminal and there are now 4 fd's (seeing a pattern here). Opening a 2nd terminal there are 6 fd's open. Close that 2nd terminal 5 fd's. Connect to a remote, which automatically opens a new terminal, 8 fd's. Close the remote terminal, still 8 fd's open. Open a new remote terminal, still 8 fd's open (some re-use here?) Open 3 more remote terminals, still 8 fd's open. Go back to the original local window and open a remote project, 10 fd's open. (seems remote's share the same local fd's between terminals in one window) Close that window, create a new window and open the same remote project, 13 fd's open. Closing all open windows (but keeping vscode running) still 13 fd's open Open a new window connected to the same remote+project, 16 fd's open. Based on this pattern it looks like: - Local terminals leak 1 fd per terminal after closing - Remote terminals share the same 2 local fd's for all terminals in one window - New windows connected to the same remote get new fd's - fd's attached to remote terminals are never closed until vscode as a whole is closed.
microsoft/vscode#182212.title: vscode leaking tty fd's

This issue is being used to track generic problems with background terminals. We plan on having a holistic look at how background terminals work soon.

microsoft/vscode#260330.body:
It would be useful to support queuing prompts for the Copilot Agent chat interface, where a second prompt can be submitted while the first is still running, and automatically processed once the current task completes.

### Why this matters:

There are many scenarios where you already know what your *next* prompt will be, but you have to wait until the agent finishes its current operation before you can submit it. This interrupts workflow and slows down the feedback loop.

This queuing feature would be especially helpful when the second prompt depends on the *result or code changes* made by the first. For example:

* I ask Copilot to refactor a block of code.
* I already know I want to generate a related unit test after that refactor completes.
* Since the second prompt depends on the updated code, it's not something I can run in parallel via a second agent.
* Instead of waiting manually, I could queue the next prompt to be executed right after the first completes.

### Proposed behaviour:

* Allow a secondary prompt to be queued while a Copilot Agent is busy.
* It gets automatically submitted once the previous task finishes.
* (Optional) A visual indicator or prompt queue list would help keep track of what's pending.

This would improve usability without requiring parallel agents or manual timing.

@burkeholland
microsoft/vscode#260330.title: Allow Queued/Steering Prompts for Copilot Agent Chat

- VS Code Version: code-stable-arm64-1758714463.tar.gz (1.104, I believe)
- OS Version: aarch64 (aka arm64) RHEL8

This is a follow up to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/174505 which was closed as Chromium thus Electron didn't work correctly on Linux with 64k pages at the time. It has been fixed a while ago in https://github.com/chromium/chromium/commit/a0ac9e357ab45c53f2bb56fd73edfa61739a2867. And at some point VSCode picked up the updates and it started working on aarch64/arm64 Linux with 64-bit code - it has been reported to work in version 1.97.2. Today download of a stable build (as above) crashes again, as previously.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Get an aarch64 aka arm64 Linux system with kernel configured to use 64k pages, for example RHEL 8:
```
$ uname -m
aarch64
$ getconf PAGESIZE
65536
```
2. Download and untar code-stable-arm64-1758714463.tar.gz.
3. Try running `VSCode-linux-arm64/bin/code` and see how it crashes:
```
$ VSCode-linux-arm64-1758714463/bin/code --version
VSCode-linux-arm64-1758714463/bin/code: line 62: 81351 Trace/breakpoint trap   (core dumped) ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 "$ELECTRON" "$CLI" "$@"
$
```

In comparison, working version 1.97.2:

```
$ VSCode-linux-arm64-1.97.2/bin/code --version
1.97.2
e54c774e0add60467559eb0d1e229c6452cf8447
arm64
$
microsoft/vscode#268354.title: VSCode doesn't start on aarch64/arm64 Linux systems using 64k pages

microsoft/vscode#269006.body:
Tasks ends up holding on to terminal instances indefinitely, leaking a bunch of memory.

Examples:

This listener is disposed if the terminal is disposed, but a reference to it is kept indefinitely as it's not deleted via `this._store.delete`:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/50e37a458d877100ed90fe48f0319b88a8effd5a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/tasks/browser/terminalTaskSystem.ts#L1566-L1569

This type of thing happens in several places where the terminal is registered against `TerminalTaskSystem` but never released:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/50e37a458d877100ed90fe48f0319b88a8effd5a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/tasks/browser/terminalTaskSystem.ts#L1410

Needs a proper audit by capturing heap snapshots to chase down all the leaks.
microsoft/vscode#269006.title: Tasks leaks terminals

microsoft/vscode#274118.body: Type: Feature Request # Proposed Enhancement for GitHub Copilot: Native Browser Integration ## Background Currently, GitHub Copilot's browser support relies on external **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** tools for browser operations. This setup leads to several challenges: - **Inconsistent Implementation**: Different tools have varying levels of support for MCP, leading to unreliable functionality. - **Reduced Efficiency**: Switching between tools or relying on external dependencies disrupts the developer's workflow. - **Fragmented User Experience**: Lack of seamless integration between Copilot and browser-based development tasks. ## Proposed Solution To address these challenges, the following enhancement is proposed: ### Native Browser Integration Implement native browser integration directly within GitHub Copilot, enabling developers to: 1. **Interact with Page Elements Directly**: Developers can directly manipulate page elements (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) within the Copilot interface, allowing for immediate changes and testing without needing to toggle between tools. 2. **Capture Screenshots for Visual Debugging**: Capture full-page or partial screenshots of the web application, making visual debugging faster and more intuitive. 3. **Access Browser Console Logs in Real-Time**: Developers can view and interact with real-time console logs, allowing for faster identification of issues such as JavaScript errors or network requests. 4. **Streamline Web Development Workflows**: With native integration, developers can perform all critical web development and debugging tasks within a single environment, eliminating the need to switch between multiple external tools. ## Benefits - **Unified Developer Experience**: A single interface for writing code, interacting with the browser, and debugging will streamline workflows and improve productivity. - **Improved Efficiency**: Native support will reduce the overhead of managing multiple tools and context switching, leading to a faster development process. - **Better Debugging**: Real-time access to browser logs and screenshots will allow for quicker identification and resolution of issues. ## Conclusion By implementing native browser integration directly into GitHub Copilot, the platform can provide a more cohesive, efficient, and productive environment for web development and debugging. This change would eliminate the dependency on third-party MCP tools and provide a seamless experience that combines the power of AI assistance with browser-based development. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.106.0-insider (ec7b5e7fa4f16991c7024a4224b790436d15a789, 2025-10-30T10:33:03.362Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2
microsoft/vscode#274118.title: Add Native Browser Integration to GitHub Copilot for Improved Web Development Experience

microsoft/vscode#274403.body:
Tracks migrating our chat implementation to use uris instead of string ids to identify chat sessions internally. Uris are preferred as they can correctly support contributed providers 

This is a pretty major change so it will broken up into smaller steps. I've done but lot of work trying to get the basics setup but now need help finishing up in individual areas 

- [x] @mjbvz Add initial support for session uris: fb6055cb587de8c95dbd6e1619edaf77ec987ced  
- [x] @mjbvz Update IChatService to use uris: db5ee1e0f4b6d7d5e8fc880f4096116655d915bc 
- [x] @mjbvz Lots of plumbing through of session uris: eef25fc6d68facf70b457602ec10b7805fede0c6 3bb2ff6262ea3f52723f14bdd1ce533872b430cb a1f7be35bf26d658c9fc7383ecbd7726babc0ecd d9d1b03c6b6496e12fa724a2513ed0d79f6eceff

- [x] @joshspicer  Convert the `clearChatEditor` function. We definitely should not be using the forChatSessionTypeAndId call I added but I'm not really sure what we should be doing instead 

    Blocks removing `forChatSessionTypeAndId` and `LocalChatSessionUri.parse`

- [x] @osortega  Convert the hybrid session stuff in `getHybridSessionsForProvider`

- [x] @osortega  Remove the use of fake `IChatSessionItem` entries with `id: "show-history"` and `id: "workbench.panel.chat.view.copilot"`

    Blocks removing `IChatSessionItem.id`

- [x] @roblourens `IChatAgentRequest.sessionId`

- [x] @connor4312 Convert `IChatEditingSession.chatSessionId`

- [x] @justschen Convert `IChatChangesSummary.sessionId`

- [ ] @aeschli Adopt in code mapper. Specifically for `ICodeMapperRequest.chatSessionId`
    
    We also need to remove  `MappedEditsRequest.chatSessionId` from `src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.mappedEditsProvider.d.ts`

- [x] @Tyriar Adopt for the terminal tool (`IChatTerminalToolInvocationData.sessionId` plus `ChatTerminalToolProgressPart` and `RunInTerminalTool`)
    
    Blocks removing `IChatResponseViewModel.sessionId` and `IChatRequestViewModel.sessionId`

- [x] @bhavyaus Adopt for `ManageTodoListTool`

- [x] @connor4312 `ChatResponseResource`
- [x] `IExecution.sessionId` @meganrogge 
- [x] `IMcpToolCallContext.chatSessionId` @connor4312 
- [x] `IToolInvocationContext.sessionId` @mjbvz 
- [x] `IAgentTitleBarStatusService` @joshspicer 

- [x] `getChatSessionIdForInstance` @meganrogge 

- [x] Inline edits @jrieken             
      https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/ec514175b20738ca44d2db3e69e93dd8168079cd/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatEditing/chatEditingTextModelChangeService.ts#L263


## Migration
In most cases, the fix is to use the uri associated with the session instead of the id. This is usually pretty straightforward, although it sometimes requires plumbing the sessionResource through a number of functions

A few gotchas/tips:

- Do not construct or parse the session uris yourself

- Make sure to use `isEqual` to compare the uris. Don't use `===`

- If the session id was being used as a key in a map, you'll likely want to switch to use a `ResourceMap`

- Use `LocalChatSessionUri.forSession` to create a URI if you only have a string id. Keep in mind this only works properly for local sessions, so we generally want to move away from this and just pass the uris around directly 

- Use `chatSessionResourceToId` to go from a uri to an old session id string. This should only be used when you need to preserve the old sessionId for telemetry or persistent storage
microsoft/vscode#274403.title: Migrate chat implementation  to use URIs instead of string ids to track sessions

microsoft/vscode#284024.body: (Edit: this is triggered by using `when` clauses on MCP servers - see comment at https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/284024#issuecomment-3805155635) I saw this once myself before (where every tool from the contributed Dart MCP server showed up twice), but wasn't able to reproduce after running the command to clear the tool cache, so I didn't raise it. However, @cdeil filed https://github.com/Dart-Code/Dart-Code/issues/5847 where the problem is much worse than I saw: The server is only registered once via an `McpServerDefinitionProvider` (which is added to the extensions `context.subscriptions` so it's disposed correctly), and it always has the same ID. I'm not sure (perhaps @cdeil can confirm) that there is only a single instance of the server being spawned, and I suspect these other tools are from the cache. @cdeil could you run the "MCP: List servers" command from the command palette when in this state and post what you see here? And perhaps also the output of running `code --status | grep mcp` in the terminal.

microsoft/vscode#285172.body: Many Agent Skills hosts (for example: Claude Code, Codex) make the skills available for explicit invocation as `/commands`, in addition to implicit invocation by the model. This is useful, since in some cases skills really function as commands, or packaged prompts, and as a user it is convenient to invoke them explicitly, and benefit from the host's completion of commands (I get a list of available commands - "prompts" in VSCode lingo - after I first type `/` in the chat input box. Expected behaviour: - I have a skill, `foo-barify` - It can be invoked by the model by identifying that I am asking for foo-barifying behaviour, based on the skill description - **I can invoke the skill explicitly by typing `/foo-barify with x = a and y = 7 and with full test coverage`** - When I type `/` into the chat box, `/foo-barify` appears in the list of prompts/commands available for invocation, alongside `.prompt.md` items.

Tracking issue for the experimental `http.systemCertificatesNode` setting that enables loading system certificates using Node.js built-in support.

## Background
- Node.js 22+ includes built-in support for loading system certificates
- This is controlled by the `http.systemCertificatesNode` setting (currently defaults to `false`)

## Status
- [ ] Gather telemetry on certificate loading (`additionalCertificates` event)
- [ ] Evaluate enabling by default

## Related
- #277838 - Disable loading system certificates from Node.js by default
- #277064
- microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#6543
microsoft/vscode#287739.title: Track loading system certificates with Node.js built-in support

microsoft/vscode#288489.body:
**# Copilot Chat sessions unexpectedly lost; sidebar cannot recover active conversations**

## Description

While using Copilot Chat in VS Code, I have repeatedly encountered unexpected session loss. The behavior includes:

- Active conversations disappearing without warning  
- The Copilot Chat sidebar failing to retain or restore the lost sessions  
- No record of the missing sessions appearing in the history  
- Loss of important context and work in progress

The issue occurs randomly and without a clear trigger. It has happened several times and has caused significant disruption to my workflow.

## Environment

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.2026011603  
- VS Code Version: 1.109.0-insider (user setup)  
- OS Version: 1656700d1db06e50d22e733b890f2d29c23f70d9  
- Feature Used: agent  
- Selected Model: Claude Opus 4.5

## Impact

- Multiple critical sessions were lost and could not be recovered  
- Important AI-generated content and context were permanently lost  
- Development flow was interrupted  
- Reliability of Copilot Chat is affected

## Troubleshooting Attempts

- Restarted VS Code  
- Reinstalled the Copilot Chat extension  
- The issue continues to occur

## Logs

[output.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24682170/output.log)
microsoft/vscode#288489.title: Copilot Chat sessions unexpectedly lost;sidebar cannot recover active conversations  ## Description

microsoft/vscode#288564.body:
Background/async terminals are owned explicitly by this agent session and common that the right thing to do it to allow an agent session to clean it up after it's done. For example:

1. Agent launches a server in the background, if it needs to relaunch the server or kill it permanently, currently it needs to kill the process from another terminal
2. Agent launched a long running compile task that completes, it cannot kill the process so the terminals stack up

There are risks associated with doing this:

- Lesser models may get confused and/or abuse this
- The background terminal may disappear while the user is looking at it
microsoft/vscode#288564.title: Add tool for killing background terminals

microsoft/vscode#288565.body: Currently when an agent launches a background terminal like a watch task it can only check the output. This results in it doing interesting workarounds like trying to run `echo "finished"` or `sleep n` to wait for it. A tool to await specific terminal(s) to either complete (in the case of a long running compile) or wait for some specific timeout would help prevent these workarounds from being used.
microsoft/vscode#288565.title: Add tool to await existing background terminal(s)

microsoft/vscode#288848.body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/18fd0633-b607-4c8b-a077-8570efe001bb

I was about to file an issue that `See more` doesn't work at all... and then I saw the PR editor open up. This is particularly bad if the PR description label is faded out, where `See more` has a lot of user expectations to just be instant and expand the container to show the text. `See more` doesn't do that at all here.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f6b08210-4356-4620-83b9-6e724359049d)

I suggest:
- Replace `See more` with a `Open PR` **button** that opens the PR editor
- Opening the PR editor needs to be instant, not delayed. The loading progress needs to be in the editor, not invisible.
- If we still want a `See more` link, it should do one thing and do it well: expand the container to make the entire text visible.
microsoft/vscode#288848.title: Clicking `See more` on a PR reference in chat has no progress UX

microsoft/vscode#289753.body: DONE DONE COULD NOT LOCATE EXISTING BUG DONE, tested insiders v 1.109.0 DONE Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: YES - VS Code Version: Version: 1.108.2 (Universal) - OS Version: MacOS Sequoia 15.7.3 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Set setting 'breadcrumbs.icons' to false 2. Use command palate/keyboard shortcut for 'Focus and Select Breadcrumbs' to select a symbol. 3. Note that the breadcrumb picker menu is put in the far upper left corner and renders under the traffic light icons. 4. You can alternatively reproduce this in a slightly different manner by clicking on a symbol breadcrumb which renders at the very top of the screen, rather than under the breadcrumb row. To see the correct behavior, either navigate with keyboard to the file/folder level of breadcrumb, where the menu renders underneath the appropriate breadcrumb, or enable icons, where the menu renders underneath the appropriate breadcrumb for symbols and folders. I've attached two screenshots of the poor renders, as well as a video that demonstrates the bug in the first half, and the correct rendering with the breadcrumbs.icons set to true in the second half. I've attached an example markdown file, though I've also tested this with a typescript file, so I suspect it's a language agnostic bug. My vscode settings: ``` json { "telemetry.telemetryLevel": "off", "editor.minimap.enabled": false, "chat.commandCenter.enabled": false, "chat.disableAIFeatures": true, "breadcrumbs.icons": false //This is the relevant setting, as enabling it causes the breadcrumb menu to render correctly regardless of position. } ``` Video of bug: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b65a97b-c28e-40d0-9bac-85a43746ee23 [sampleDocWithMultipleSymbols.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24807978/sampleDocWithMultipleSymbols.md) Poor Render on Keyboard Command/Navigation: Poor Render on Symbol Click:

microsoft/vscode#290207.body: - Scroll down to render a long response which is still in progress - Feel a pause and see layout thrashing in the perf trace The completed thinking parts are rendered as expanded, so they don't benefit from lazy rendering, then when we finalize them, we measure the contents which forces a relayout even though the contents are not visible, and this is all very expensive. cc @justschen

The link `https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/setup/enterprise#_configure-allowed-extensions` which is referenced multiples times across the code base (example: [extensionManagement.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/extensionManagement/common/extensionManagement.ts#L726) L.726), which was broken earlier last week, now redirect to https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/enterprise/overview#_configure-allowed-extensions.



I believe it should redirect directly to the subsection https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/enterprise/extensions#_configure-allowed-extensions

microsoft/vscode#290529.body:
Some agents are only meant to be used as subagents, while other agents are not to be used as subageent,

We want to extend the `infer` to a string enum with the values `all`, `user`, `agent` and`hidden`.
boolean `true` and `false` can still be read, they map to `all` and `user`.
`all` means the agent will be available as custom agent in the custom agent picker and can be used as subagent.
`user` means it is available as custom agent in the agent picker
`agent` means it can only be used as subagent but the subagent tool
`hidden` means it is neither in the custom agent picker not can be picked by the subagent tool. 

If infer is not set, it defaults to `user`.
microsoft/vscode#290529.title: Allow custom agents to be user-hidden by default

microsoft/vscode#290647.body: ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/286170 - [x] anyOS @eleanorjboyd - [x] anyOS @bhavyaus Complexity: 4 [Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23290647%0A%0A&assignees=joshspicer) --- ### Pre-requisites - _For partner agents_: Must feature flagged into the GitHub. Contact Josh, who will contact GitHub to get you flagged in. - Once FF'd in, you need to self-enable here: https://github.com/settings/copilot/coding_agent ### Steps 1. Open a personal GitHub repo 2. Open chat 3. Swap to 'cloud' 4. Observe options selectable for the cloud agent NOTE: The three pickers are `[Partner Agent] [Custom Agent] [Model]`. These pickers will dynamically hide if there are no options to pick from. For example, if you don't have a [custom agent (`apple_developer_docs` for me)](https://github.com/joshspicer/tankgame/blob/main/.github/agents/apple-docs.agent.md) checked into your GitHub repo, it wouldn't show up here: Selecting a 'partner agent' other than 'copilot' will HIDE the custom agent and model pickers. Those are not suppported. Some examples of expected views: Please test out this feature by sending cloud agent jobs for your personal repo. ### Reporting issues Please try to include (or DM to Josh) your _trace_ logs from `Output > github copilot chat`.
microsoft/vscode#290647.title: Test: [Cloud Agent] Model and Partner Agents

microsoft/vscode#290675.body:
Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/118932

- [x] AnyOS @dbaeumer 
- [x] AnyOS @joshspicer 

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23290675%0A%0A&assignees=dmitrivMS)

---

VB Language Spec:
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/visual-basic/language-reference/

Use various language constructs and verify auto-indentation works as expected.
Verify both formatting and Enter behavior after a line.

**NOTES**
- There will be edge cases as it's still regex based and not semantic
- Colorization is VB is broken in many cases due to very stale TextMate grammar

More detailed test plan is below.

### Setup
1. Create/open a `.vb` file
2. Set `editor.autoIndent` to `full`

### Test 1: Block Statement Indentation (Increase)
Type a block-opening statement and press Enter:
```vb
If True Then↵
    |  ' cursor should be indented
```

Verify indent increases after statements like:
- Control flow: `If...Then`, `Else`, `ElseIf`, `Select Case`, `Case`
- Loops: `While`, `For`, `Do`
- Declarations: `Sub`, `Function`, `Class`, `Module`, `Property`
- Others: `Try`, `Catch`, `Finally`, `With`, `Using`

### Test 2: Block Statement Indentation (Decrease)
Type a closing statement and verify automatic de-indent:
```vb
If True Then
    ' some code
End If   ' should align with If
```

Verify de-indent for:
- `End If`, `End Sub`, `End Function`, `End Class`, `End Module`, `End Select`, etc.
- `Else`, `ElseIf`, `Case`, `Catch`, `Finally`
- Loop terminators: `Loop`, `Next`, `Wend`, `Until`

### Test 3: Single-Line Statements (No Extra Indent)
Verify no indent increase when block opens and closes on same line:
```vb
If x = 1 Then Return End If↵
|  ' cursor same level, no indent
```

### Test 4: onEnter After End Statements
Press Enter on a line after an End statement:
```vb
End If↵
|  ' cursor should NOT be additionally indented
```

### Test 5: onEnter on Blank Line After Block Terminators
After an End statement, press Enter on a blank line:
```vb
End Sub
↵   ' blank line, press Enter
|   ' should maintain indentation, not increase
```

### Test 6: Case Insensitivity  
Verify indentation works regardless of casing:
```vb
IF TRUE THEN
    ' indented
END IF  ' de-indented
```

### Test 7: Comments After Statements
Block-opening keywords followed by comments should still indent:
```vb
If True Then ' comment↵
    |  ' should be indented
```
microsoft/vscode#290675.title: [Test Plan Item] Add language configuration file for visual basic

microsoft/vscode#290687.body:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/204696

- [x] Windows @aeschli x64
- [x] Windows @bamurtaugh arm64

Complexity: 4

Authors: @deepak1556 


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23290687%0A%0A&assignees=deepak1556)

---
## Context

VS Code on Windows 11 now integrates with the modern context menu (right-click menu) in File Explorer. This feature enables the "Open with Code" option to appear directly in the top level of Windows 11 context menu without requiring users to click "Show more options" to access the legacy context menu.

## Prerequisite

1. Download the User setup from [here](https://builds.code.visualstudio.com/builds/stable?commit=59224bb916e2e8af89a0640cd168b6773101dcc0&dev=true)
2. Run the installer and check the **Add 'Open with Code' action to Windows Explorer file context menu** option
3. Complete the installation

## Testing

### Opening Files

1. Right-click on any file from the Windows file explorer
2. Select "Open with Code"
3. Verify VS Code opens with the selected file

### Opening Folders

1. Right-click on a folder from the Windows file explorer
2. Select "Open with Code"
3. Verify VS Code opens with the folder as the workspace

### Run as Administrator Scenarios

> Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/180065

1. Navigate to VS Code installation folder (e.g., `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` or `C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code`)
2. Right-click `Code.exe` and select "Properties"
3. Click on the "Compatibility" tab
4. Check "Run this program as an Administrator"
5. Click "OK" to save
6. Navigate to a folder from the Windows file explorer
7. Right-click on the folder and select "Open with Code"
8. Verify a UAC (User Account Control) elevation prompt appears
9. Click "Yes" on the UAC prompt
10. Verify VS Code opens correctly with administrator privileges, you should see `[Administrator]` in the window title
11. Right-click on a file and select "Open with Code"
12. Verify a UAC prompt appears and VS Code opens correctly after approval

### Run as Administrator - UAC Denied

1. Configure VS Code to run as Administrator (as above)
2. Right-click on a folder and select "Open with Code"
3. When the UAC prompt appears, click "No"
4. Verify VS Code does not open (expected behavior - access denied)

### Uninstallation

1. Uninstall VS Code
2. Open the Windows file explorer and right-click on any file
3. Verify "Open with Code" no longer appears in the modern context menu
4. Right-click on any folder
5. Verify "Open with Code" no longer appears in the modern context menu

### Upgrade Scenarios

1. Install a previous version of VS Code from [here](https://builds.code.visualstudio.com/builds/stable?commit=c9d77990917f3102ada88be140d28b038d1dd7c7&dev=true)
2. Verify context menu entry appears under "Show more options" menu
3. Upgrade to the new version, use `Developer: Apply Update` command from within VS Code
4. Verify "Open with Code" now appears in the top level context menu
5. Verify old context menu entries are cleaned up (no duplicates)

microsoft/vscode#290699.body:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update to latest VSCode Insiders version within VSCode Insiders.
2. Restart Windows after update.
3. Right-click in a folder, notice there is no context menu option still.

I also tried reinstalling the VSCode Insiders (System) installation, I ensured the options to add the context menu options to explorer were enabled and completed the installation. To give the best chance of working, I rebooted again and then started VSCode. Again, right-clicking in a folder shows no context menu option for 'Open with VSCode' still.

I am making a new issue as the previous tracked ones are closed/locked for some reason.

- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/287832
- Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/260389

_**Note:** The old context menu entry is also missing. I have no means to open VSCode from any right-click menu in Windows currently._

## Full About Info

```
Version: 1.109.0-insider (system setup)
Commit: b074b32e5a00d95510b5471393a62c9be38c127e
Date: 2026-01-26T17:05:31.575Z
Electron: 39.2.7
ElectronBuildId: 13098910
Chromium: 142.0.7444.235
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
```

## Workaround

At this point in time, this feature has been broken for nearly 3 years. For those that do not want to continue to wait for a fix, this post contains a workaround that is currently working:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/204696#issuecomment-3720762337

I just installed this and it restored the menu option without issue.
microsoft/vscode#290699.title: VSCode Insiders - Windows 11 Context Menu Still Missing (System Installer)

microsoft/vscode#290972.body:
Testing #290583

**Bug:** When the LLM calls `run_in_terminal` with an extremely large timeout value like `1e+26`, the command output appears broken.

**Reproduction:**
1. Ask the agent to run a command with an extremely high timeout
2. The model called the tool with:
```json
{
  "command": "sleep 10 && echo done",
  "timeout": 1e+26
}
```

**Expected:** Command runs normally and outputs "done" after 10 seconds

**Actual:** Output is truncated/broken - "done" was never printed:
```
Note: The tool simplified the command to ` sleep 10 && echo done`, and this is the output of running that command instead:
rosswollman@rwoll:~/code/tpi-test $ 
```

**Suggestion:** Add validation/clamping for timeout values to prevent integer overflow or other issues with absurdly large numbers.
microsoft/vscode#290972.title: Terminal tool breaks with extremely large timeout values (e.g., 1e+26)

microsoft/vscode#291328.body:
## Description

The current question carousel UI in chat uses native radio buttons for single-select questions and checkboxes for multi-select. This creates an inconsistent experience compared to other VS Code selection UIs like QuickPick.

## Proposal

- **Single-select questions**: Replace radio buttons with a list-style selection UI similar to the QuickPick widget
- **Multi-select questions**: Replace plain checkboxes with a list-style UI with checkboxes (like QuickPick multi-select mode)

## Benefits

1. **Visual consistency** - Aligns with the established QuickPick UI pattern users are familiar with
2. **Better UX** - List-style selection provides a cleaner, more modern appearance
3. **Platform consistency** - QuickPick styling is consistent across platforms while native form controls vary
4. **Unified design language** - Both single and multi-select follow the same list-based interaction model

## Current behavior

Radio buttons are rendered for single-select and checkboxes for multi-select, as seen in the implementation files:
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/test/browser/widget/chatContentParts/chatQuestionCarouselPart.test.ts`
- `src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.chatParticipantAdditions.d.ts`
microsoft/vscode#291328.title: Question carousel UI: Use list-style selection instead of radio buttons/checkboxes

microsoft/vscode#291487.body:
## Problem

When a model provides single/multi select options in the question carousel, sometimes all the provided options are unsuitable for the user's needs. Without a freeform input option, users are forced to select one of the bad options or skip the question entirely.

Previously, the freeform input was only shown when the `allowFreeformInput` API property was set to `true` by the model. This created a poor user experience when the model didn't enable this option.

## Solution

Always show the freeform input field for single-select and multi-select questions, regardless of the `allowFreeformInput` API property value. The API property is kept for backwards compatibility but is now ignored.

### Behavior

- **SingleSelect**: If a freeform value is provided, only that value is used (ignoring the selected radio option)
- **MultiSelect**: Both selected checkbox values and freeform input are combined together

This ensures users always have the ability to provide their own answer when the predefined options don't meet their needs.
microsoft/vscode#291487.title: Question carousel should always show freeform input

microsoft/vscode#291553.body:
The agent sessions viewer needs reliable timings to function. We see issues for local sessions where:
* timings are reported differently when a chat is opened
* chats are showing as unread that were previously marked as read

Lets try to trace the flow of how timings from a local chat get into the session:

The local sessions provider fills the `timing` from the `IChatDetail.timing` property here:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/987f736f422fb1f88df13cf613aa216065b0ae8a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/localAgentSessionsProvider.ts#L120

Now we seem to have 3 origins of the timing depending on if the model is alive or not:
* https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/987f736f422fb1f88df13cf613aa216065b0ae8a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts#L1821
* https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/987f736f422fb1f88df13cf613aa216065b0ae8a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts#L388-L392
* https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/987f736f422fb1f88df13cf613aa216065b0ae8a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts#L409-L413

Going into the details of a `ChatModel`, the `created` time has a fallback to `Date.now()` that I find suspicious, esp. if the chat model was around earlier:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/987f736f422fb1f88df13cf613aa216065b0ae8a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts#L1911

The other 2 times (response started, response finished) seem to mainly be looking at the last response and taking their time values, but I see many more `Date.now()` usages in `chatModel.ts`.

I am asking for a check that we are confident the time values are stable and are not changing when a history item becomes "alive".
microsoft/vscode#291553.title: Ensure chat timings are sound

## Description

The text area component used in the question carousel (ask_questions tool) has a minimum height issue. When freeform input is enabled, the text area appears with insufficient minimum height.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Trigger a question carousel dialog that allows freeform text input
2. Look at the "Or enter your own:" text area at the bottom
3. Observe the text area has a minimum height issue

## Expected Behavior

The text area should have an appropriate minimum height for comfortable text entry.

## Actual Behavior

The text area appears with a height constraint issue affecting its usability.

## Screenshots

microsoft/vscode#291739.body:
- VS Code Version: 1.108.2
- OS Version: Windows 11

This was noticed by the fact that diagnostic entries of the file being closed doesn't get removed from Problems View, if a file was opened from extension by `vscode.workspace.openTextDocument()`, which differs from opening files from Explorer panel, in which case diagnostic entries are removed on file close.

Reproduction steps:
1. Open a file which contains problems, using `vscode.workspace.openTextDocument()` call from extension
2. Ensure that problems appear in Problems View
3. Close file
4. Observe that diagnostics remains in Problems View
5. Additionally, if you give VS Code several minutes to idle, at some point it triggers resource cleanup, removing giagnostics from Problems View unexpectedly

Also, when using a watcher task with problems matcher, diagnostics does not get replaced by task's diagnostics outputs when file is closed.
Not necessarily, you can check the video I have posted in https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/256118#issuecomment-3645844851 to demonstrate diagnostics vanishing (ignore that there I blame `getDiagnostics()` for this, since now it is clear that the source is different). Another place I mention this issue is https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/290278#issue-3853689195

I tried to fix it and found something, but I'm not sure how to complete it. Here are my results
1. First, I have found that for the file opened from EH, [ModelService.onModelRemoved](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/fd3565eb6bd06e688a98cabd029088c16390e62e/src/vs/editor/common/services/modelService.ts#L94) is not fired on close
2. The reason for this turned out to be that the `reference.counter` of model equals `2` when the [dispose](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/fd3565eb6bd06e688a98cabd029088c16390e62e/src/vs/base/common/lifecycle.ts#L699) is called on close, and that differs for the files opened from Explorer panel, which is `1` in that case
3. Found that, what adds additional reference, is [ExtHostDocuments._proxy.$tryOpenDocument](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/fd3565eb6bd06e688a98cabd029088c16390e62e/src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostDocuments.ts#L90), which end up here [const ref = await this._textModelResolverService.createModelReference(uri);](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/fd3565eb6bd06e688a98cabd029088c16390e62e/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDocuments.ts#L286) and is seems that it doesn't get disposed on close
4. I have tested what if I reduce counter by 1 manually for the file opened from EH. It gets disposed then, and diagnostics removed from Problems View as it should, but diagnostics does not get replaced by running task's problem matcher outputs
microsoft/vscode#291739.title: Bug: documents opened by EH: vscode.workspace.openTextDocument() are not disposed correctly

Type: Bug **Type:** Bug **Description:** When an MCP server returns image content with a correct `mimeType` (e.g., `image/jpeg`), VS Code displays the image but labels it as "file.bin" instead of inferring a proper filename with extension (e.g., "image.jpeg" or "file.jpg"). **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. Configure an MCP server that returns `ImageContent` with a data URI 2. The server returns a response like: ```json { "type": "image", "data": "", "mimeType": "image/jpeg" } ``` 3. Invoke the MCP tool from Copilot Chat 4. Observe the image is displayed but labeled as "file.bin" **Expected Behavior:** The image should be labeled with a filename that reflects the MIME type, such as "image.jpeg" or "file.jpg". **Actual Behavior:** The image is labeled as "file.bin" regardless of the MIME type provided. **Additional Context:** The MIME type is correctly included in the response (`image/jpeg`). VS Code should be able to infer the file extension from the MIME type (e.g., `image/jpeg` → `.jpg`, `image/png` → `.png`). microsoft/vscode#292011.body:
Refs: #292003, #291351

Complexity: 2

Authors: @karthiknadig

---

## Overview

This test plan covers the freeform input functionality and summary display for the `askQuestions` tool. The freeform textarea is now always displayed for single-select and multi-select question types, allowing users to provide custom answers.

### Key Behaviors

- **Single-select questions**: Freeform input takes priority over list selection. If user types in the freeform textarea, the selection is ignored.
- **Multi-select questions**: Freeform input is combined with checkbox selections. Both are included in the answer.
- **Freeform textarea auto-resizes**: As the user types, the textarea grows dynamically (up to 200px max height).
- **Summary display**: After submission, answers are displayed in a summary view with proper text wrapping.

## Setup

1. Install Latest insiders GitHub Copilot Chat extension
2. Open the Chat panel
3. Ensure `askQuestions` tool is enabled
4. Have the model ask you questions (e.g., "Quiz me on this repo. 4 questions max")

## Testing

### Freeform Input

- [ ] Verify freeform textarea is displayed below the options for single-select questions
- [ ] Verify freeform textarea is displayed below the options for multi-select questions
- [ ] For single-select: Select an option, then type in the freeform textarea - verify only the freeform value appears in summary (selection is ignored)
- [ ] For multi-select: Select some options AND type in the freeform textarea - verify both selections and freeform value appear in summary (comma-separated)
- [ ] Type multiple lines in the freeform textarea - verify it auto-resizes as you type
- [ ] Verify freeform textarea respects maximum height (doesn't grow beyond ~200px)

### Summary Display

- [ ] Submit answers and verify the summary displays correctly
- [ ] Verify question labels don't show double colons (e.g., should show "Question:" not "Question::")
- [ ] Resize the window to be narrow - verify summary text wraps properly instead of being cut off
- [ ] For single-select with freeform: verify freeform value is shown as the answer (not in parentheses)
- [ ] For multi-select with freeform: verify freeform value is listed alongside selected options

### Navigation and Persistence

- [ ] Navigate between questions using arrow buttons
- [ ] Verify freeform text is preserved when navigating back to a previous question
- [ ] Verify both selection and freeform text are preserved after navigation

### Keyboard Navigation

- [ ] Tab through the carousel elements
- [ ] Verify textarea accepts multi-line input with Enter key
microsoft/vscode#292011.title: Chat `askQuestions` Tool - Freeform Input and Summary

microsoft/vscode#292042.body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.108.2
- OS Version: Ubuntu 25.10

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Cause sticky scrolling for something that can be folded. Like some big JSON.
2. Scroll down enough that the top line of the foldable/collapsable region becomes part of the sticky scroll header area.
3. Observe that either the folding control doesn't appear in the line margin, or if it does, clicking it does nothing.

Related on Stack Overflow: [Code folding buttons disappeared in sticky scroll in VS Code](https://stackoverflow.com/q/79869755/11107541)

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Summary

Unified Quick Access should preserve the legacy prefix behavior, and consistently support both `>` (Commands) and `<` (Sessions) for quick mode switching.

## Problem

In legacy Quick Access, typing `>` immediately switched to Commands. In Unified Quick Access this no longer works reliably, and there’s no symmetric `<` behavior for Sessions.

## Expected Behavior

- Typing `>` switches to **Commands**
- Typing `<` switches to **Sessions**
- Removing the leading prefix returns to **Files**
- Input should normalize to a single prefix character (`>` or `<`)

## Why this matters

Prefix switching is a fast, established keyboard workflow. Restoring it (and making it symmetric) keeps Unified Quick Access consistent and efficient.

## Notes

This matches legacy Quick Access behavior for `>` and extends it consistently with `<` for Sessions.
microsoft/vscode#292202.title: Unified Quick Access: Restore Prefix Switching With > (Commands) and < (Sessions)

microsoft/vscode#292367.body:
## Summary
Implement comprehensive accessibility help (Alt+F1) across all VS Code find and filter experiences to improve screen reader user experience with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver.

## Problem
Screen reader users currently struggle to discover:
- Available keyboard shortcuts in find/filter dialogs
- How to navigate between search fields and results
- Differences between "find" and "filter" operations
- Context-specific behaviors (e.g., Terminal find reversed Enter key)

## Solution
Add Alt+F1 accessibility help across 7 find/filter contexts:

### 1. Foundation & Infrastructure
- Add `AccessibilityVerbositySettingId.Find` configuration
- Extend `AccessibleViewProviderId` for find/filter contexts
- Add accessibility help trigger to `FindController`
- Register contributions in all find/filter features

### 2. Accessibility Help Content (7 providers)
- **Editor Find/Replace** - Comprehensive help with current search status
- **Terminal Find** - Terminal-specific behaviors (reversed Enter)
- **Webview Find** - Extension-provided find with limitations
- **Output Panel Filter** - Filter vs. find distinction
- **Problems Panel Filter** - Quick filtering in error lists
- **Debug Console Filter** - REPL filtering
- **Search Across Files** - Multi-file search help

### 3. ARIA Hints & Bug Fixes
- Add aria-description hints: "Press Alt+F1 for accessibility help"
- Fix spurious "No results" announcements on empty search
- Fix stale aria-label updates when widget hidden
- Prevent double-speak with 1-second reset timeout

## Files Changed
- 10 foundation/infrastructure files
- 7 accessibility help provider files
- 6 widget files with ARIA improvements
- ~3,000 lines total

## Testing
All find/filter dialogs tested with:
- NVDA on Windows
- JAWS on Windows  
- VoiceOver on macOS

## Related
Addresses accessibility gaps in find/filter workflows identified by screen reader users.
microsoft/vscode#292367.title: Add Accessibility Help System for Find/Filter Dialogs

microsoft/vscode#292375.body:
## Summary

This issue tracks ARIA improvements and bug fixes for find/filter widgets to enhance the screen reader experience.

## Problem Statement

Currently, find widgets have several accessibility issues:

1. **Spurious "No results" announcements** - The find widget announces "No results" even when the search string is empty, causing confusing screen reader output
2. **Stale aria-label updates** - The widget updates aria-labels even when not visible, which can interfere with screen reader focus
3. **Missing accessibility help hints** - Users have no indication that accessibility help is available (Alt+F1)
4. **Double-speak issue** - Hints can be announced multiple times in rapid succession

## Proposed Changes

### Bug Fixes (findWidget.ts)

1. **Guard search result announcements** - Only announce results when a search string is present:
   ```typescript
   // Before: Always announced results
   // After: Only announce when search string exists
   if (this._state.searchString) {
       this._updateSearchResultAria();
   }
   ```

2. **Check widget visibility** - Only update aria-label when widget is visible:
   ```typescript
   if (!this._isVisible) {
       return;
   }
   ```

### ARIA Hint Additions (simpleFindWidget.ts)

Add accessibility help hints to all find widgets inheriting from SimpleFindWidget:

1. **Add announcement tracking flag**:
   ```typescript
   private _accessibilityHelpHintAnnounced: boolean = false;
   ```

2. **Add IConfigurationService and IAccessibilityService** for settings checks

3. **Announce hint pattern** - Announce "Press Alt+F1 for accessibility help" on focus:
   - Only announce once per session
   - Uses 1-second reset timeout to prevent double-speak
   - Only when accessibility.verbosity.find setting is enabled
   - Only when screen reader is active

### Files Changed

| File | Change Type | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `src/vs/editor/contrib/find/browser/findWidget.ts` | Bug Fix | Guard against spurious announcements |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/codeEditor/browser/find/simpleFindWidget.ts` | Enhancement | Add ARIA hints base implementation |
| `src/vs/workbench/browser/parts/views/viewFilter.ts` | Enhancement | Add ARIA hints to tree filter widgets |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/search/browser/searchWidget.ts` | Enhancement | Add ARIA hints to search widget |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/find/browser/terminalFindWidget.ts` | Wiring | Add service dependencies |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/webviewFindWidget.ts` | Wiring | Add service dependencies |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No "No results" announcement when search input is empty
- [ ] No aria-label updates when find widget is hidden
- [ ] Screen reader users hear "Press Alt+F1 for accessibility help" once when focusing find input
- [ ] Hint only announces when accessibility.verbosity.find setting is enabled
- [ ] No double-speak of the hint

## Related

- Depends on: #292367 (Accessibility Help System infrastructure)
- PR: (to be created)

## Testing

1. Enable screen reader (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver)
2. Open find widget (Ctrl+F)
3. Verify hint is announced once
4. Close and reopen - verify hint announces again
5. Clear search input - verify no "No results" announcement
microsoft/vscode#292375.title: Add ARIA hints and fix spurious announcements in Find/Filter widgets

microsoft/vscode#292736.body:
`chat.tools.terminal.autoReplyToPrompts` does not work if the there's case differences in some scenarios.

Commit: f3d99dee2f4c63061a08f828e0ca6b6acf6ac0f8
Ext Version: 0.37.2026020304

## Steps to Repro:

1. Add `"chat.tools.terminal.autoReplyToPrompts": true` to your settings.
2. Save the following into a file in your workspace:

   ```bash
   #!/bin/bash
   # Simple script that asks for user confirmation before proceeding
   
   echo "This script will create a file called 'confirmed.txt'"
   echo ""
   read -p "Do you want to continue? (Y/n): " answer
   
   if [ "$answer" = "y" ] || [ "$answer" = "Y" ]; then
       echo "Success!"
       echo "User confirmed the action." > confirmed.txt
       echo "File 'confirmed.txt' has been created."
       exit 0
   else
       echo "Operation cancelled by user."
       exit 1
   fi
   ```
3. Change to Claude Sonnet 4, and send prompt of `Run the script confirm_action.sh in the terminal`.
4. Observe that you are still presented with a confirmation prompt, despite having auto-reply enabled.
5. Repeat a few times b/c LLMs.
6. Change the `Y/n` to `y/n` try again a few times and observe that auto-reply works as expected.

## Additional Notes

This is a hypothesis, though pretty reliably reproducible in my experience. To better confirm (and for future debugging), please log:

1. whether an option was autoReply eligigble (if autoReply is set)
2. the options the LLM extracts, etc.
microsoft/vscode#292736.title: [terminal] chat.tools.terminal.autoReplyToPrompts flakes on case sensitivity of the presented options

microsoft/vscode#292917.body:
The chat question carousel will soon be more accessible thanks to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/292911.

However, it still lacks an aria label and one can not move focus via keyboard to the container.

Additionally, focus automatically moves to the first response, which is against our usual pattern and could be very disruptive and confusing for screen reader users.

I'm thinking when in screen reader mode, we should not move focus. Since they're alerted, we should add a command and keybinding to focus the first response.

cc @accesswatch, @jooyoungseo, @rperez030
microsoft/vscode#292917.title: Improve chat question carousel accessibility

- VS Code Version: 1.110.0-insider (commit b8124edbda5cdacc07ca6d35b9e03e20fdb736a4)
- OS Version: macOS Tahoe 26.2

**Steps to Reproduce:**

- Run `npx @vscode/test-web --extensionDevelopmentPath=. --browserType=none` with any web extension
- Open browser console


**Expected:** 
- No errors, extension host initializes normally

**Actual:** 
- Console shows this error and nothing loads
```
ERR [invokeFunction] unknown service 'IExtHostHooks': Error: [invokeFunction] unknown service 'IExtHostHooks'

    at KW.initialize (extensionHostWorkerMain.js:119:9739)
```
It appears that `IExtHostHooks` is missing in the web extension host worker context. If I use `--commit bdd88df003631aaa0bcbe057cb0a940b80a476fa` things do work as usual.

microsoft/vscode#293049.body: Type: Bug Here's where I observed it, but I don't think it is caused by an extension bug. 1. Be on Windows. 1. Have Docker Desktop installed. 1. Have the Container Tools extension installed. 1. Have no folders open. 1. In the Containers view open the context menu of a running container and choose `Attach Shell` :bug: in the new Terninal session that opens, the message is: ``` * Executing task: & 'C:\Program Files\Docker\Docker\resources\bin\docker.EXE' exec -it [REDACTED_TOKEN] bash * The terminal process failed to launch: Cannot launch a terminal process in an untrusted workspace with cwd C:\Users\JohnM and userHome c:\Users\JohnM. ``` As well as erroneously comparing the cwd and userHome values case-sensitively the message misleads by referring to an "untrusted workspace". The code implies it should say "empty workspace". VS Code version: Code 1.109.0 (bdd88df003631aaa0bcbe057cb0a940b80a476fa, 2026-02-04T02:01:38.288Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

microsoft/vscode#293277.body:
## Follow-up to #285172

With SKILL.md files now working as `/commands`, discovery is limited to the workspace-root `.github/prompts/` folder (or paths configured via `chat.promptFilesLocations`). Monorepo setups where packages have their own skills are not well served.

### Problem

In a monorepo with multiple packages, teams often want package-specific skills:

```
my-monorepo/
├── .github/prompts/           # Workspace-wide prompts ✅ discovered
├── packages/
│   ├── frontend/
│   │   └── .github/prompts/   # Frontend-specific skills ❌ not discovered
│   │   └── .copilot/skills/   # Alternative location ❌ not discovered
│   └── backend/
│       └── .github/prompts/   # Backend-specific skills ❌ not discovered
```

Users currently must either:
- Flatten all skills into the root `.github/prompts/` (loses package context)
- Manually add each path to `chat.promptFilesLocations` (tedious, doesn't scale)

### Proposed behavior

When the user is working with files in a subdirectory, VS Code should automatically discover prompt/skill files from `.github/prompts/` (or `.copilot/skills/`) directories up the directory tree from the active file, in addition to the workspace root.

For example, editing `packages/frontend/src/App.tsx` would discover skills from:
1. `packages/frontend/.github/prompts/`
2. `.github/prompts/` (workspace root)

Skills from narrower scope could take precedence when names collide.

### Additional considerations

- **Performance**: Directory scanning should be cached and use file watchers, not walk the tree on every `/` keystroke
- **Namespace collisions**: Define clear precedence rules (narrower scope wins, or prefix with package name)
- **Settings integration**: `chat.promptFilesLocations` should support glob patterns (e.g., `packages/*/.github/prompts`) as an alternative to automatic discovery
- **Agent Skills spec alignment**: The [Agent Skills spec](https://agentskills.io) and Claude Code both support nested `.claude/skills/` discovery — aligning on convention would improve portability

### Prior art

- [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) auto-discovers `.claude/skills/` in subdirectories
- VS Code already has precedent for nested config discovery (e.g., `.vscode/` in multi-root workspaces, nested `tsconfig.json`)
microsoft/vscode#293277.title: Customizations: Nested directory discovery for monorepo support

microsoft/vscode#296955.body:
## Bug

The terminal tool (`run_in_terminal`) corrupts **any multiline command exceeding 1024 bytes**. After byte 1024, content wraps around and replays earlier buffer content, destroying the remainder of the command and leaving the shell stuck in `quote>` or `heredoc>` mode.

## Minimal Reproduction

Ask Copilot to run this in the terminal (19 lines × 55 chars + wrapper = 1077 bytes):

```
echo 'L01 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L02 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L03 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L04 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L05 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L06 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L07 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L08 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L09 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L10 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L11 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L12 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L13 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L14 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L15 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L16 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L17 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L18 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
L19 aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa' | wc -c
```

**Expected**: Outputs `1064`
**Actual**: L19 becomes garbled (`L19 aaaaaL02 aaa...`), closing `'` is lost, shell stuck at `quote>`

Reduce to 18 lines (1021 bytes) → works perfectly. The boundary is exactly 1024 bytes.

## Verified Facts

| Test case | Result |
|---|---|
| 18 lines × 55 chars (1021 bytes), multiline | ✅ Works |
| 19 lines × 55 chars (1077 bytes), multiline | ❌ Corrupts at byte 1024 |
| Same 20-line content via `$'...\n...'` (1156 bytes, single-line) | ✅ Works |
| Single line of 2000+ chars | ✅ Works |
| `cat << 'EOF'` heredoc > 1024 bytes | ❌ Corrupts at byte 1024 |
| `python3 -c "..."` multiline > 1024 bytes | ❌ Corrupts at byte 1024 |

The corruption only affects **multiline** commands (containing literal newline characters) and always occurs at **exactly byte 1024**.

## Why This Is a Serious Problem

The failure mode is **cascading and difficult to recover from**:

1. **Silent corruption**: The tool reports success ("The tool simplified the command to...") but the shell receives garbled data. The agent has no way to detect this before it happens.

2. **Shell gets permanently stuck**: The closing delimiter (`'`, `"`, `EOF`) is lost in the corruption, so the shell enters `quote>` or `heredoc>` mode. Every subsequent command the agent sends is swallowed as continuation of the broken quote — the terminal is bricked until a new one is opened.

3. **Extremely common trigger**: 1024 bytes is a very low threshold. Any of these routine operations can exceed it:
   - `gh issue create --body '...'` or `gh pr create --body '...'` with a detailed description
   - Heredoc writes (`cat > file << 'EOF'`)
   - Inline Python/Node scripts (`python3 -c "..."`)
   - Any multiline shell script pasted to the terminal

4. **Hard to diagnose**: The garbled output looks like random gibberish — fragments of earlier content mixed with later content. Without knowing about the 1024-byte boundary, this appears to be a non-deterministic, unreproducible glitch. (We initially filed and closed a report as "unreproducible" before discovering the byte-count trigger.)

5. **Recovery makes things worse**: When the agent sees an error or stuck shell, it typically retries. But the terminal is stuck in quote mode, so retries just feed more text into the broken quote, creating increasingly bizarre output and wasting tokens.

## Root Cause Hypothesis

The terminal tool uses a different input mechanism for multiline commands (likely bracketed paste or line-by-line writing to the PTY) compared to single-line commands. This multiline path appears to use a **1024-byte buffer** — a classic `PIPE_BUF` or PTY canonical-mode buffer size — that wraps on overflow rather than flushing and continuing.

## Environment

- VS Code Insiders
- macOS
- zsh
- Copilot agent mode (Claude)
microsoft/vscode#296955.title: Terminal tool corrupts multiline commands exceeding 1024 bytes

microsoft/vscode#298433.body:
Type: Bug **Title**: `[Agent] get_terminal_output accepts invalid IDs silently; ToolCallingLoop stops with reasons=undefined` **Extension**: GitHub Copilot Chat `0.37.8` **VS Code**: (your version) **OS**: Windows 11 --- **Bug 1 -- `get_terminal_output` does not validate ID before tool is offered to the model** The model is passed `get_terminal_output` as an available tool with no schema constraint on the `id` field. The model infers plausible-sounding IDs (`"1"`, `"pwsh"`, `"last"`, `"0"`, `"terminal1"`, `"the most recent terminal"`, `"auto"`, `"91"`) because the tool description does not state that only IDs returned by `run_in_terminal(isBackground=true)` are valid. **Evidence from session `20260224T190400` log (24,535 lines, Feb 24-25 2026):** ``` 25 [error] entries across 18+ hours, every distinct invalid ID the model guessed: 2026-02-24 19:36:34 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "1"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-24 21:28:57 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "last"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-24 22:14:53 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "1"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-24 23:53:34 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "pwsh"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-25 08:58:31 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "the most recent terminal"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-25 11:17:37 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "4"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-25 11:20:31 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "auto"}: Invalid terminal ID 2026-02-25 14:46:48 [error] get_terminal_output {"id": "91"}: Invalid terminal ID ``` **Fix suggestion**: Add to the tool description: *"The `id` must be the exact opaque string returned by `run_in_terminal` when `isBackground=true`. Terminal names, labels, or integers are not valid."* --- **Bug 2 -- `[ToolCallingLoop] shouldContinue=false, reasons=undefined` (208 occurrences)** The stop hook fires and halts the tool loop, but surfaces `reasons=undefined` to the model. The model receives no explanation for why it was stopped, so it retries the same blocked call or silently abandons the task with no user-visible error. **Evidence:** ``` 208 occurrences in same session, first at: 2026-02-24 19:11:00 [info] [ToolCallingLoop] Stop hook result: shouldContinue=false, reasons=undefined 2026-02-24 19:11:51 [info] [ToolCallingLoop] Stop hook result: shouldContinue=false, reasons=undefined 2026-02-24 19:15:21 [info] [ToolCallingLoop] Stop hook result: shouldContinue=false, reasons=undefined ... (205 more) ``` **Impact**: The model interprets the silent stop as a transient failure and retries. User sees the agent "spinning" or abandoning tasks for no stated reason. Correlates with 217 `Request ID for failed request` entries in the same session. **Fix suggestion**: When `shouldContinue=false`, the `reasons` field should always be a non-null string. If the reason is unavailable, surface `"stop hook returned no reason"` rather than `undefined`. --- **Attach**: `C:\Users\marco.presta\AppData\Roaming\Code\logs\20260224T190400\window1\exthost\GitHub.copilot-chat\GitHub Copilot Chat.log` Extension version: 0.37.9 VS Code version: Code 1.109.5 (072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622, 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631 Modes:

microsoft/vscode#298507.body:
I am a user of Github Copilot (via GitHub Education Pack). My **GitHub Copilot Chat History** is **Not Showing**, although it was working perfectly fine, earlier. I don't think there are exact steps to reproduce, but I'll try to mention what I think caused it. I have attached all logs from my VSCode, since I didn't know if there is any particular log file to attach.

Furthermore, I followed this too https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-release/issues/872 but no luck. I didn't uninstall the GitHub Copilot Extension for a reason. I had 5 VSCode windows opened. 1 window is of a Remote Instance connected via SSH, and it had GitHub Copilot History as well. So if it was extension related, then it should occur only in the local extension but all my chats are gone, including the ones which I accessed via the same VSCode, but for the remote instance session! 
I am pissed due to this, as the chat history had important stuff. There is no option to sync or export the chat easily, and even though I was planning to export it as .json, I was unsure if it will download all chats together or 1 chat individually, even if there are multiple chats in a session/window.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.23.2
- VS Code Version: 1.96.2
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100 (Windows 11 Home - Version 24H2)
- Logs: [vscode_logs.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18239726/vscode_logs.zip)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. My PC apps were a bit stuck, so I just ended VSCode from Task Manager, and relaunched it 
2. I have checked my chat sessions, but all sessions are gone!

I have done closing the VSCode in past too, via Task Manager, or even via direct shutdown, but all the windows, along with their Github Copilot chats, are always restored. Even for the remote instance, which I usually shut down and connect again daily, the chats stay there! So, I am surprised how all chats vanished! I didn't close sessions/windows for VSCode, so I won't lose the chat history and now it's gone! Please Help! Let me know if you need anything else.
microsoft/vscode#298507.title: Github Copilot Chat History not showing

microsoft/vscode#298982.body: Type: Bug Opening a Simple Browser tab and trying to access a local dev server over https using a self signed certificate is blocked with an error page saying: Failed to Load Page ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID (-202) URL: `https://my-custom-domain.dev.localhost:portNumber/path` If I open the same url in MS Edge, I get a warning but I can click through to continue anyway because I trust my local dev server. But in the VS Code Simple Browser tab there's no way to continue - I'm just stuck here. I've tried using mkcert, overriding VS Code arg variables in system user settings as well as workspace settings, clicking in the page and typing `thisisunsafe` - nothing works. There's no way to bypass this page and continue to my local dev server over https. VS Code version: Code 1.109.5 (072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622, 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:
microsoft/vscode#298982.title: ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID for localhost HTTPS in Simple Browser Tab despite working fine in MS Edge

microsoft/vscode#299021.body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In the docs, we refer to keybindings using the syntax `kb({command ID})`. The mapping is done based on default keybinding JSON files, which are manually exported with each release for each platform.

This feature adds a command-line option to generate the default keybindings JSON files for all three platforms.
microsoft/vscode#299021.title: Add command-line option to export default keybindings for all platforms

microsoft/vscode#299486.body: My boy wants to file a complaint about his `run_in_terminal` tool silently truncating outputs when he calls it with a timeout. What I *sometimes* see in chat is the agent trying to run some command, then getting confused about missing or truncated outputs. We had a bad case of this today here: I told the agent to run some tests with/without timeouts and different delays to try to reproduce this problem. This is what it came up with, seems plausible: # `run_in_terminal` timeout silently truncates output with no signal to agent ## Environment - VS Code: 1.109.3 (user setup, commit b6a47e94e326b5c209d118cf0f994d6065585705, 2026-02-12) - Electron: 39.3.0, Chromium: 142.0.7444.265, Node.js: 22.21.1 - GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.37.9 (github.copilot-chat) - Model: Claude Opus 4.6 - OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045, PowerShell 7.5.4 ## Description When the agent's `run_in_terminal` tool is called with a non-zero `timeout` and the command takes longer than the timeout, the tool silently returns partial output with **no indication** that a timeout occurred. The agent cannot distinguish "command completed with this output" from "timeout hit, command still running." Additionally, the still-running command's output accumulates in the terminal buffer and **pollutes subsequent `run_in_terminal` calls**, which dump the entire backlog. **Not the same as #296887** — that issue is about the 10s `NoneExecuteStrategy` heuristic. This issue is about the explicit `timeout` parameter on the tool itself. ## Minimal reproduction ### Command finishes within timeout → works ``` command: echo "A"; Start-Sleep -Seconds 5; echo "B" timeout: 20000 ``` Agent receives: `A`, `B` ✅ ### Command exceeds timeout → silent truncation ``` command: echo "A"; Start-Sleep -Seconds 5; echo "B" timeout: 3000 ``` Agent receives: `A` only ❌ No error, no timeout indicator — looks like the command completed normally. ### No timeout → always works ``` command: echo "A"; Start-Sleep -Seconds 12; echo "B" timeout: 0 ``` Agent receives: `A`, `B` ✅ (even for long-running commands) ### Subsequent call shows polluted buffer After the truncated 3s-timeout call above, the next `run_in_terminal` invocation returns all previously accumulated output from the still-running terminal — including the missed `B` from the timed-out command, mixed with historical output. ## Full test matrix | Sleep duration | Timeout param | Agent sees both A and B? | |---------------|---------------|-------------------------| | 2s | 5000 | ✅ Yes | | 5s | 20000 | ✅ Yes | | 8s | 0 | ✅ Yes | | 12s | 0 | ✅ Yes | | 5s | 3000 | ❌ Only A | | 10s | 3000 | ❌ Only A | ## Expected behavior When the timeout expires, the tool should return a **clear signal** that a timeout occurred — for example: - A distinct field/flag in the response (e.g., `"timedOut": true`) - A message appended to the output (e.g., `[Timeout expired after 3000ms — command may still be running]`) Currently, the tool description says _"stop tracking the command after this duration and return the output collected so far"_ — but the agent receives no way to detect that this happened. ## Impact - Agent makes incorrect decisions based on partial output (thinks builds/tests succeeded when still running) - Terminal buffer pollution causes confusing outputs on subsequent calls - Agent generates incorrect analysis/reports from truncated output - Workaround: always use `timeout: 0`, which defeats the purpose of the parameter
microsoft/vscode#299486.title: run_in_terminal timeout silently truncates output with no signal to agent
