microsoft/vscode#300439.body: Type: Feature Request When an agent session already changed a file, inline chat now always queues new messages into that session instead of making changes in isolation. This ensures that the full context is used and is also useful when reviewing agent edits. This shouldn't be mandatory. What if I need a fast inline edit? I should be able to choose the old behaviour if I want to. If I want my selection queued in the chat, I should explicitly press a button for it, or you should use a different shortcut instead of simply blocking inline edits completely. VS Code version: Code 1.111.0 (ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb, 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

microsoft/vscode#300480.body: - Select Copilot CLI as agent type, worktree as isolation mode, and default approvals - Ask the Copilot CLI to edit package.json - ❓ Copilot CLI makes the edit without asking for permission, since it's working on a worktree - If I try the same flow with workspace instead of worktree, or with the local agent instead of Copilot CLI, then I get prompted as expected with default approvals selected Should we provide some sort of indicator to users somewhere about this behavior? microsoft/vscode#300795.body:
## Bug Report

### Description

The SCM count badge in the activity bar includes resource counts from hidden repositories (e.g., worktrees created by background agent sessions). This is inconsistent with the fixes applied in #286127 and #286429, which correctly excluded hidden repositories from the Repositories menu and the Changes view, but did not address the count badge.

### Root Cause

In `src/vs/workbench/contrib/scm/browser/activity.ts`, the `SCMActiveRepositoryController` uses `Iterable.filter(this.scmService.repositories, r => r.provider.isHidden !== true)` to compute repositories for the count badge. This manual filtering does not use `scmViewService.visibleRepositories`, which is the canonical source for visible repositories and already accounts for hidden state.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace with one or more Git repositories
2. Create a background agent session that uses a worktree (this adds a hidden repository)
3. Make changes in the worktree repository
4. Observe the SCM count badge in the activity bar

### Expected Behavior

The count badge should only reflect resource counts from **visible** repositories, consistent with the Repositories menu and the Changes view.

### Actual Behavior

The count badge includes resource counts from hidden repositories (e.g., agent worktrees), inflating the displayed count.

### Additional Context

- Related: #286127 (hidden repos in Repositories menu - fixed)
- Related: #286429 (hidden repos in Changes view - fixed)
- The fix is to replace `Iterable.filter(scmService.repositories, ...)` with `scmViewService.visibleRepositories` and listen to `onDidChangeVisibleRepositories`.

### VS Code Version

1.112.0-dev (commit `755a4199cc9`)

### OS Version

macOS Darwin 25.2.0
microsoft/vscode#300795.title: SCM count badge includes hidden repositories

microsoft/vscode#300857.body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes (all other extensions are disabled) - VS Code Version: 1.111.0 - OS Version: Windows 11 and Arch Linux Steps to Reproduce: 1. Disabled all other extensions including Github Copilot Chat (or install a fresh vscode instance). 2. Enable for example rust-analyzer or anything that marks errors in the sourcecode (testet also with javascript files/lints) 3. Hover over the error. You can see "✨ Fix" to invoke copilot: 4. Clicking "Fix" doesn't do anything 5. Install "Github Copilot Chat" (current version 0.39.0) 6. Go back to the error and hover over it. "✨ Fix" is gone. 7. Restarting doesn't help.

microsoft/vscode#301169.body: We should indicate the zoom percent as a temporary overlay or some other option. ## Reference **Edge:** **Chrome:** **VS Code workbench:** When zoomed in: When zoom at 100%: When zoomed out: ## Current limitations We're limited with overlays because of the way Integrated Browser uses a floating WebContentsView so we might need to get creative to avoid having any overlays overlapping with the page itself. The way Find in Page feature works around the overlap issue is to push the whole page with a bar that appears: but that might not be ideal for displaying zoom since it will only be shown temporarily and will cause the page to shift.
microsoft/vscode#301169.title: Browser Zoom: Display zoom percent

microsoft/vscode#301458.body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.40.2026031301 - VS Code Version: VS Code Insiders 1.112.0-insider (Universal) - OS Version: macOS 15.6.1 (24G90) (Apple Silicon, MacBook Pro M4 Pro) - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Copilot Chat (model selector) - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus 4.6 / Claude Sonnet 4.6 / GPT-5.4 - Logs: No specific error logs appear in the output panel. (It's empty.) The issue occurs in the model selector UI where certain models show "Contact your admin" despite the account being a personal GitHub account. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open VS Code Insiders. 2. Open Copilot Chat panel. 3. Click the model selector dropdown. 4. Observe that several models (Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4) appear but cannot be selected. 5. The UI displays the message "Contact your admin". Expected behavior: Models should either be selectable or clearly indicate that they are unavailable due to plan restrictions. NOTE: I was able to use Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4. Since yesterday, I am unable to use them. Actual behavior: The UI displays "Contact your admin" even though the account is a personal GitHub account (not part of an organization) with GitHub Education verification and Copilot Pro usage available. Additional context: - The GitHub account is a **personal account**, not part of any organization or enterprise workspace. - The account is **GitHub Education verified** and has access to Copilot Pro features. - Premium usage quota has **not been reached** (approx. 68% used at the time of the screenshot). - Some models appear in the dropdown but are **not selectable** and display the message "Contact your admin". - This message is confusing because the account does not have an admin or organization controlling Copilot settings. Question: Is this expected behavior due to **plan restrictions or staged model rollout**, or is the UI incorrectly showing enterprise policy messaging for personal Copilot users? Screenshots are attached showing the model selector and Copilot usage panel.
microsoft/vscode#301458.title: Copilot Chat shows “Contact your admin” for models on personal GitHub account (VS Code Insiders)

microsoft/vscode#301564.body:
### Problem
In Accessible View, file references are shown without their corresponding file paths in scenarios where the visual UI includes enough context to identify the file/location only when hyperlinked/clickable.

For screen reader users, this removes critical context and makes it difficult to understand or navigate the referenced location.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a workflow that surfaces file/location references in the UI (for example, references/links that visually imply a file target).
2. Open Accessible View for that content.
3. Compare the visual content vs Accessible View text output.

### Actual Behavior
Accessible View text omits file paths for references that should carry path context.

### Expected Behavior
Accessible View should include file path context (and, where available, line/column) for file references so the text representation is semantically equivalent to the visual content.

### Why this matters
Accessible View is intended to provide a complete textual representation of rich/visual content. Missing file paths creates an accessibility regression because important navigation context is lost.

### Suggested direction
- Ensure accessible-view content providers include path metadata when generating text for file references.
- Preserve parity between visual link context and accessible text output.
microsoft/vscode#301564.title: Accessible View should include file paths when presenting file references

microsoft/vscode#301568.body:
### Accessibility problem
Some UI context is currently implicit (visual-only) and is not obvious to screen reader users. The related controls should communicate this context directly in accessible names.

### Requested improvements
1. Make the implicit context explicit in the element aria-label so screen reader users get the same meaning that sighted users infer visually.
2. Include the relevant setting in the aria-label/help text so users know how to disable this behavior from Settings.
3. Add a clear aria-label for the **Remove** button.

### Why this matters
When context is only conveyed visually, screen reader users can miss key meaning and available actions. Including the disable-setting hint in the accessible text also improves discoverability and control.

### Repro/observation
- In the UI shown in the attached screenshot, key context appears to be implicit.
- Screen reader output does not sufficiently expose that context.
- The Remove control should have an explicit and descriptive aria-label.

### Expected behavior
- Aria-label includes the missing context.
- Aria-label/help exposes which setting disables this behavior.
- Remove button has a descriptive aria-label (for example, what is being removed).

### Notes
- This request is specifically about improving accessibility semantics and discoverability for screen reader users.
- Screenshot referenced from report context.
microsoft/vscode#301568.title: Improve screen reader labels: expose implicit context, include disable-setting hint, and label remove button

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

## Built-in Profile template "Python" fails to create — Create button disabled

**VS Code Version:** 1.111.0 (ce099c1, arm64)
**OS:** macOS 15 (Darwin 25.2.0, Apple Silicon)

### Problem

Selecting the built-in "Python" template via `Profiles: Create Profile` fails silently — the **Create button stays disabled** and the profile is never registered.

### Root Cause

The CDN-hosted template at `https://main.vscode-cdn.net/core/python.code-profile` includes two Copilot extensions:

```
github.copilot        (GitHub Copilot)
github.copilot-chat   (GitHub Copilot Chat)
```

When VS Code attempts to install these extensions during profile creation, it gets stuck at "Started installing extensions" and **never reaches "Finished"**. Meanwhile, the "Doc Writer" template (which does not include these extensions) completes successfully.

### Evidence from renderer.log

```
09:25:26 Importing Profile (Python): Started installing extensions.   ← never finishes
09:26:02 Importing Profile (Python): Started installing extensions.   ← retry, still stuck
09:28:00 Importing Profile (Python): Started installing extensions.   ← stuck again

09:29:00 Importing Profile (Doc Writer): Started installing extensions.
09:29:17 Importing Profile (Doc Writer): Finished installing extensions. ← completes fine
```

Additionally, there are repeated JSON parse errors from `getChildrenFromProfileTemplate`:

```
Expected property name or '}' in JSON at position 3 (line 2 column 2)
    at JSON.parse (<anonymous>)
    at KF.apply (workbench.desktop.main.js:1798:29635)
    at Iy.getChildrenFromProfileTemplate (workbench.desktop.main.js:4912:60906)
```

And 404 errors from Extension Host when resolving the template:

```
[Extension Host] GET /gists/python.code-profile - 404
```

### Side Effect

Each failed attempt creates an orphan profile directory under `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/profiles/` that is never registered in `storage.json`. After multiple attempts, I had **29 orphan directories** with only 1 registered profile.

### Workaround

Download the template file from `https://main.vscode-cdn.net/core/python.code-profile`, remove the `github.copilot` and `github.copilot-chat` entries from the `extensions` field (and related references in `globalState`), then import via **Profiles: Import Profile** from file.

### Suggested Fix

1. Update the CDN template to remove or replace the deprecated `github.copilot` extension entry
2. Handle extension installation failures gracefully — a single extension failure should not block the entire profile creation

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes
microsoft/vscode#301645.title: Built-in Profile template "Python" fails to create — Create button disabled

microsoft/vscode#301748.body: At some point an update was made so that prompts do not fill out your chat box when you press tab to complete one. We have prompts shared between our team and we can't expect people to run other's shared prompts without being able to read them. On top of that, many prompts are setup so that you finish it out yourself, and it's hard to do that if you can't read the prompt It would be ideal if it was an option. Please add the ability to turn off anything you add, as this is a terrible feature for any reasonable developer. Prompts are auto-loaded from MCPs and extensions, this is dangerous really. Cursor does this too, and it's not usable as a developer tool.

microsoft/vscode#302289.body:
## Problem

The `anonymizeFilePaths()` function in `src/vs/platform/telemetry/common/telemetryUtils.ts` redacts file paths in stack traces to strip PII. It relies on "cleanup patterns" (built from `piiPaths`) to preserve VS Code's own paths so they remain readable in telemetry. However, the **browser telemetry service** at `src/vs/workbench/services/telemetry/browser/telemetryService.ts` does **not** set `piiPaths`, unlike the electron-browser version which uses `getPiiPathsFromEnvironment(environmentService)`.

In web environments (e.g. Codespaces), stack traces contain HTTPS URLs like:
```
at x3t._delegate (https://codespace-host.github.dev/static/build/bundle.js:1:200953)
```

The `fileRegex` in `anonymizeFilePaths` matches the URL path portion and redacts it to `<REDACTED: user-file-path>`, making the entire stack trace useless for debugging:
```
at x3t._delegate (https:/<REDACTED: user-file-path>:1:200953)
```

## Root Cause

`BrowserWorkbenchEnvironmentService` doesn't implement the `IPathEnvironment` interface (`appRoot`, `extensionsPath`, `userHome`, `tmpDir`, `userDataPath`), so `getPiiPathsFromEnvironment()` can't be used. The browser telemetry config at line 117 of `src/vs/workbench/services/telemetry/browser/telemetryService.ts` omits `piiPaths` entirely. Without `piiPaths`, the only cleanup pattern is the static `/(vscode-)?file:\/\/.*?\/resources\/app\//gi` which doesn't match HTTPS URLs.

## Impact

All error telemetry from VS Code web (vscode.dev, Codespaces) has fully redacted stack traces, making it impossible to identify which VS Code source file an error originates from.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Add the web app's origin/base URL as a cleanup pattern so HTTPS bundle URLs are preserved, or
2. Adjust `anonymizeFilePaths` to not treat HTTPS URLs as file paths worth redacting (e.g. skip URLs matching `https?://`)

## Related

Found while investigating #301212.
microsoft/vscode#302289.title: Error telemetry stack traces are fully redacted in web environments (Codespaces)

microsoft/vscode#302316.body:
VS Code currently supports `vscode://` URI handlers for extension installation and `vscode:mcp/install` for MCP server installation. We should extend this pattern to provide comprehensive URL handlers for plugin installation that support:

1. **Installing plugins from a GitHub repo** — A URL handler that accepts a GitHub repository URL and installs the plugin directly
2. **Installing plugins from any source** — Support for arbitrary plugin sources beyond the default marketplace
3. **Adding new marketplaces** — A URL handler to register additional plugin marketplaces
4. **Combo URL handler** — A single URL that can both add a marketplace and install a specific plugin from it in one step
microsoft/vscode#302316.title: URL Handlers for Plugin Installation from Multiple Sources and Marketplaces

Currently, the run actions list is sorted by most recently used, and I wonder if it'd be better to have it stay sorted in some fixed way? I mention this because I actually fat-fingered the one action a couple times, because my muscle-memory was trying to click the action at the top, based on expecting it to be there.

As an aside, I wonder whether we want to annotate/distinguish worktree-setup actions vs. others, since in practice, the user wouldn't commonly run worktree setup actions manually?

microsoft/vscode#302453.body: When grouping the sessions list by repository, it creates an `Other` group for sessions that it can't identify a repo for. That said, since the groups appear to be sorted by the recency of their sessions, the `Other` group can be intermingled into the repo list, which feels a bit surprising, especially since these are almost always sessions that were created from external tools (?). I wonder whether it would make sense to always place `Other` at the bottom, so that we give priority to known repos?

microsoft/vscode#302576.body:
## Bug

When installing the [context7 plugin](https://github.com/upstash/context7) via **Chat: Install Plugin From Source**, the plugin's skills and commands load correctly, but the MCP server defined in the plugin's `.mcp.json` does not get registered.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Run **Chat: Install Plugin From Source** and enter `https://github.com/upstash/context7`
2. The plugin installs successfully — skills (e.g., `context7-mcp`) and commands appear in chat
3. The MCP server defined in the plugin's `.mcp.json` does not appear in the MCP server list and is not available as a tool

## Expected

The MCP server (`context7`, type `http`, url `https://mcp.context7.com/mcp`) should be registered and available after plugin installation, just like the plugin's skills and commands are.

## Plugin structure

The plugin at `plugins/claude/context7/` contains:

```
.claude-plugin/plugin.json   # manifest
.mcp.json                    # MCP server definition
skills/                      # ✅ loads correctly
commands/                    # ✅ loads correctly
agents/
```

The `.mcp.json` uses the Open Plugins / Claude Code format:

```json
{
  "context7": {
    "type": "http",
    "url": "https://mcp.context7.com/mcp"
  }
}
```

## System info

VS Code Insiders, v1.112 with `chat.plugins.enabled: true`
microsoft/vscode#302576.title: MCP server from context7 plugin does not load

microsoft/vscode#302579.body:
How are we supposed to ship a local MCP server that requires env variables to work? The plugin loads fine in copilot CLI.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.39.2
- VS Code Version: 1.111.0
- OS Version: macOS 26.3
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Does not matter
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Does not matter
- Logs: No

Steps to Reproduce:

Create an agent plugin, and set the `.mcp.json` to something like this;

```
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "enterprise-github": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": [
        "run",
        "-i",
        "--rm",
        "-e",
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN",
        "-e",
        "GITHUB_HOST",
        "ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server"
      ],
      "env": {
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "${ENTERPRISE_GITHUB_TOKEN}",
        "GITHUB_HOST": "https://enterprise.github.example.com"
      },
      "tools": ["*"]
    }
  }
}

```

The MCP server does load but throws 401. Works fine in copilot CLI.
microsoft/vscode#302579.title: Agent Plugin's MCP does not expand env variables

microsoft/vscode#302669.body: If you run an AI code review on your changes, and then accept/reject the resulting comments, then the button for running a review seems to become disabled. In practice, I think it's pretty common for folks to run a couple passes of review--particularly with different models--just to make sure everything looks clean. And based on the current UX, I wasn't sure how to do that?
microsoft/vscode#302669.title: Sessions: AI code review doesn't allow running multiple times?

microsoft/vscode#302993.body: If you click the filter icon in the sessions list, you'll see two `Local` options. That way we have three options: Copilot CLI, Claude, and Cloud? That would match the terminology and VS Code and be more direct with what these are?

microsoft/vscode#303014.body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
I find the option to group my sessions by repository to be incredibly helpful for session management and tracking. I don't think I would've discovered this option on my own without seeing other team discussions on it. It doesn't feel like users would lose much if we changed from the current `Group by Date` default: 
- Users can minimize the sessions list for specific repos, if they aren't working in them at the moment
     - I wonder if we'd want an option to pin repos, like what @bpasero added for pinning sessions?
- Sessions are still sorted by time within each repo grouping

I recognize this may be my unique preference, so also happy to keep this open for further user feedback with no further action for now, or for us to close it out and only reopen if we get other feedback.

Related https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/302453
microsoft/vscode#303014.title: Sessions: Consider making `Group by Repository` the default state

microsoft/vscode#303022.body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
I find it unexpected that launching Sessions via the dedicated app versus `Chat: Open Sessions Window` will surface different history (different sets of folders recently opened, which then leads to differences like different recent chat sessions and customizations available). Is this intended (as a means of separating the experiences, similar to how Insiders and Stable wouldn't necessarily share history), or just a point-in-time experience (and we'd expect both versions of Sessions to match over time)?
microsoft/vscode#303022.title: Sessions: Different history and options depending on how launched

microsoft/vscode#303118.body:
Type: Bug 1. Open a `fish` terminal. 2. Press ⇧⌘W to close the window. Expected: the window closes. Observed: the shortcut is sent to the terminal and nothing happens by default. I've verified that: - This happens with all extensions disabled. - This does not happen in `bash`. - In fact, it also doesn't happen when `bash` is launched from inside `fish`. - `fish_key_reader` inside the terminal reports `super-shift-w` for ⇧⌘W (but nothing for `⇧⌘P`, which correctly invokes the command palette) I suspect this is related to the fix for https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/295372 , as I'm also seeing changes in how key bindings are reported to `fish` with this week's release. microsoft/vscode#303361.body:
## Problem

When a background task's `endsPattern` matches terminal output, VS Code only uses the match as a signal that background processing has ended. Any data captured by the regex (e.g., a dynamically assigned port or URL) is discarded.

This means launch configurations that depend on dynamic values from task output (like a server URL) must hardcode values or rely on workarounds.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce `${taskVar:name}` variables that capture **named groups** from a problem matcher's `endsPattern` regex and make them available for substitution in launch configurations (and anywhere else variables are resolved).

### How it works

1. A task's problem matcher `endsPattern` uses a named capture group:
   ```json
   "endsPattern": "  current session url: (?<componentExplorerUrl>.*)"
   ```

2. When the pattern matches, the named group values are extracted and registered as contributed variables.

3. A launch configuration references the captured value:
   ```json
   "url": "${taskVar:componentExplorerUrl}"
   ```

### Naming convention

The `${taskVar:name}` syntax follows VS Code's existing `${name:arg}` convention (like `${env:PATH}`, `${config:setting}`, `${command:id}`).

## Implementation

- `WatchingProblemCollector.tryFinish()` extracts `matches.groups` from the `endsPattern` match
- `IProblemCollectorEvent` carries captured variables in the `BackgroundProcessingEnds` event
- `TerminalTaskSystem` registers captured variables via `IConfigurationResolverService.contributeVariable()`
- `VariableKind.TaskVar` added for completeness

## Example

```jsonc
// tasks.json
{
    "label": "Start Server",
    "type": "shell",
    "command": "npm start",
    "isBackground": true,
    "problemMatcher": {
        "background": {
            "activeOnStart": true,
            "beginsPattern": ".*Starting.*",
            "endsPattern": "Server listening on (?<serverUrl>http://localhost:\\d+)"
        }
    }
}

// launch.json
{
    "name": "Debug in Browser",
    "type": "chrome",
    "request": "launch",
    "url": "${taskVar:serverUrl}",
    "preLaunchTask": "Start Server"
}
```
microsoft/vscode#303361.title: Support `${taskVar:name}` variables from problem matcher named capture groups

microsoft/vscode#303601.body:
## Bug

The `sanitizeId` function in `settingsTreeModels.ts` uses a regex without the `g` (global) flag:

```typescript
function sanitizeId(id: string): string {
    return id.replace(/[\.\/]/, '_');
}
```

Without the `g` flag, `String.prototype.replace` only replaces the **first** match. Since settings IDs contain multiple dots (e.g., `root.editor.font.size`), only the first dot gets replaced:

```
Input:  "root.editor.font.size"
Actual: "root_editor.font.size"   // only first dot replaced
Expected: "root_editor_font_size" // all dots replaced
```

## Fix

Add the `g` flag to the regex: `/[\.\/]/g`

## Location

- File: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/preferences/browser/settingsTreeModels.ts`, line 755
microsoft/vscode#303601.title: sanitizeId regex missing global flag - only first dot/slash is replaced in settings tree IDs

This is a replacement for the individual quickpicks traditionally accessed via the 'Gear' in chat. You may also open from the command palette (`> Chat: Open Customizations (Preview)`) ## Steps Please treat this TPI as exploratory and use it as you would in daily programming. Additionally, please validate the following: 1. Validate any relevant customizations you use are picked up. a. You may want to install a couple extension (e.g Azure Tools extension, which has a lot of extension-contributed agents/skills/etc) 1. Validate swapping between harnesses (Local, Copilot CLI, Claude) filters the correct customizations 1. Create some user-scoped customizations 1. Create some workspace-scoped customizations 1. Use the 'generate X' features to create a new customization 1. Note any customizaton related faeture not met by this UI. Please also test in the Sessions App (`> Chat: Open Sessions Window`). Features like swapping harnesses and extensions are not available.

microsoft/vscode#303665.body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.113.0-insider (c99f8109a67528cd9ccb6de2d513bd5a7d52aa1f 2026-03-20T09:51:41-07:00)
- OS Version: Windows 11 10.0.26200

Hi. Thanks to @jcansdale for PR #302526. It fixed a real multiline execution problem, but it also introduced a regression for `workbench.action.terminal.sendSequence`.

## Problem
The `workbench.action.terminal.sendSequence` command can no longer simulate Enter by sending `\u000D`, because any occurrence of `\r` or `\n` now triggers automatic bracketed paste wrapping.
This appears to regress the [documented behavior](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/terminal/advanced#_custom-sequence-keyboard-shortcuts):
> `sendSequence` can be used to send Arrow keys, **Enter**, cursor moves, and more.
## Repro
Bind a key to send `\u000D` to the integrated terminal:

```json
{
	"key": "ctrl+enter",
	"command": "workbench.action.terminal.sendSequence",
	"args": { "text": "echo 123\u000D" },
	"when": "editorFocus"
},
```
Pressing the key no longer behaves like Enter in Git Bash. Instead of executing the current command line, it moves to a new line / behaves as pasted input.
## Proposed fix
#303666
microsoft/vscode#303665.title: Integrated terminal: `workbench.action.terminal.sendSequence` no longer simulates Enter with `\u000D` in Git Bash

microsoft/vscode#303673.body:
The find files and find text in files tools used by Copilot chat pass glob patterns that come from the LLM. These patterns are often in a different case than the actual file extensions or paths on disk (e.g. `**/*.TSX` vs `**/*.tsx`). The tools should use case-insensitive glob matching so that results aren't silently missed due to casing mismatches.

VS Code's search infrastructure already supports an `ignoreGlobCase` option on queries. We need to:

1. Expose a `caseInsensitive` option on the `FindFiles2Options` and `FindTextInFilesOptions2` proposed APIs
2. Wire it through `extHostWorkspace` to the internal `ignoreGlobCase` query builder option
3. Have the chat search tools pass `caseInsensitive: true`

(Written by Copilot)
microsoft/vscode#303673.title: Chat search tools should use case-insensitive glob matching

microsoft/vscode#303714.body: From @roblourens ``` { "name": "run_in_terminal", "description": "This tool allows you to execute shell commands in a persistent zsh terminal session, preserving environment variables, working directory, and other context across multiple commands.\n\nCommand Execution:\n- Use && to chain simple commands on one line\n- Prefer pipelines | over temporary files for data flow\n- Never create a sub-shell (eg. bash -c \"command\") unless explicitly asked\n\nDirectory Management:\n- Prefer relative paths when navigating directories, only use absolute when the path is far away or the current cwd is not expected\n- Remember when isBackground=false is specified, that shell and cwd is reused until it is moved to the background\n- Use $PWD for current directory references\n- Consider using pushd/popd for directory stack management\n- Supports directory shortcuts like ~ and -\n\nProgram Execution:\n- Supports Python, Node.js, and other executables\n- Install packages via package managers (brew, apt, etc.)\n- Use which or command -v to verify command availability\n\nBackground Processes:\n- For long-running tasks (e.g., servers), set isBackground=true\n- Returns a terminal ID for checking status and runtime later\n\nOutput Management:\n- Output is automatically truncated if longer than 60KB to prevent context overflow\n- Use head, tail, grep, awk to filter and limit output size\n- For pager commands, disable paging: git --no-pager or add | cat\n- Use wc -l to count lines before displaying large outputs\n\nBest Practices:\n- Quote variables: \"$var\" instead of $var to handle spaces\n- Use find with -exec or xargs for file operations\n- Be specific with commands to avoid excessive output\n- Avoid printing credentials unless absolutely required\n- Use type to check command type (builtin, function, alias)\n- Use jobs, fg, bg for job control\n- Use [[ ]] for conditional tests instead of [ ]\n- Prefer $() over backticks for command substitution\n- Use setopt errexit for strict error handling\n- Take advantage of zsh globbing features (**, extended globs)", "input_schema": { "type": "object", "properties": { "command": { "type": "string", "description": "The command to run in the terminal." }, "explanation": { "type": "string", "description": "A one-sentence description of what the command does. This will be shown to the user before the command is run." }, "goal": { "type": "string", "description": "A short description of the goal or purpose of the command (e.g., \"Install dependencies\", \"Start development server\")." }, "isBackground": { "type": "boolean", "description": "Whether the command starts a background process.\n\n- If true, a new shell will be spawned where the cwd is the workspace directory and will run asynchronously in the background and you will not see the output.\n\n- If false, a single shell is shared between all non-background terminals where the cwd starts at the workspace directory and is remembered until that terminal is moved to the background, the tool call will block on the command finishing and only then you will get the output.\n\nExamples of background processes: building in watch mode, starting a server. You can check the output of a background process later on by using get_terminal_output." }, "timeout": { "type": "number", "description": "An optional timeout in milliseconds. When provided, the tool will stop tracking the command after this duration and return the output collected so far with a timeout indicator. Be conservative with the timeout duration, give enough time that the command would complete on a low-end machine. Use 0 for no timeout. If it's not clear how long the command will take then use 0 to avoid prematurely terminating it, never guess too low." } }, "required": [ "command", "explanation", "goal", "isBackground", "timeout" ] } }, ``` ... ``` 🛠️ run_in_terminal (toolu_vrtx_01WtWhpwX2sbwe9DoBejxFp1) { "command": "cd /Users/roblou/code/agent-host-protocol && npm run generate", "explanation": "Regenerate protocol docs and JSON schemas from updated types", "goal": "Generate protocol documentation", "isBackground": false, "timeout": 30000 } ``` ... ``` 🛠️ toolu_vrtx_01WtWhpwX2sbwe9DoBejxFp1 Command failed while running in sandboxed mode. microsoft/vscode#304448.body:
## Bug

In `fuzzyScorer.test.ts`, the `assertScore()` helper in the `fuzzyScore2 (multiple queries)` test contains an operator precedence bug:

```typescript
assert.ok(multiScore ?? 0 >= ((firstSingleScore ?? 0) + (secondSingleScore ?? 0)));
```

The `>=` operator (precedence 11) binds tighter than `??` (precedence 5), so JavaScript parses this as:

```typescript
assert.ok(multiScore ?? (0 >= ((firstSingleScore ?? 0) + (secondSingleScore ?? 0))))
```

When `multiScore` is a number (the normal case for a successful match), `??` short-circuits and `assert.ok(positiveNumber)` passes due to truthiness. The `>=` comparison is never evaluated.

The test claims to verify that a multi-query score is at least as large as the sum of individual query scores, but it does not actually check that.

## Verification

```typescript
const multiScore = 42;
const firstSingleScore = 100;
const secondSingleScore = 100;

// Buggy (current): multiScore ?? (0 >= 200) => 42 => truthy => passes
assert.ok(multiScore ?? 0 >= (firstSingleScore + secondSingleScore)); // passes wrongly

// Fixed: (42) >= (200) => false => fails correctly
assert.ok((multiScore ?? 0) >= (firstSingleScore + secondSingleScore)); // fails as expected
```

## Location

- File: `src/vs/base/test/common/fuzzyScorer.test.ts`, line 1242

## Fix

Add parentheses to enforce the intended grouping:

```typescript
assert.ok((multiScore ?? 0) >= ((firstSingleScore ?? 0) + (secondSingleScore ?? 0)));
```
microsoft/vscode#304448.title: Operator precedence bug in fuzzyScore2 test assertion - comparison never evaluated

microsoft/vscode#304451.body:
## Bug

In the performance view's startup metrics table, the "blocking startup" count double-counts `LifecyclePhase.Starting` and omits `LifecyclePhase.Ready` contributions.

### Current code

```typescript
// Line 218 in perfviewEditor.ts
`${(contribTimings.get(LifecyclePhase.Starting)?.length ?? 0) + (contribTimings.get(LifecyclePhase.Starting)?.length ?? 0)} blocking startup`
```

`LifecyclePhase.Starting` is used for both operands. The second should be `LifecyclePhase.Ready`.

### Evidence

The correct pattern appears later in the same file in `_addWorkbenchContributionsPerfMarksTable()` (lines 279-280), which correctly lists both blocking phases:

```typescript
md.li(`Total (LifecyclePhase.Starting): ${timings.get(LifecyclePhase.Starting)?.length} ...`);
md.li(`Total (LifecyclePhase.Ready): ${timings.get(LifecyclePhase.Ready)?.length} ...`);
```

The same method's perf marks filter also confirms both phases 1 (`Starting`) and 2 (`Ready`) are "blocking":

```typescript
e.name.startsWith('code/willCreateWorkbenchContribution/1') ||  // Starting
e.name.startsWith('code/didCreateWorkbenchContribution/1') ||
e.name.startsWith('code/willCreateWorkbenchContribution/2') ||  // Ready
e.name.startsWith('code/didCreateWorkbenchContribution/2')
```

### Impact

The "blocking startup" count shown in the performance view (Developer: Startup Performance) is incorrect -- it doubles the `Starting` phase count and misses all `Ready` phase contributions.

## Location

- File: `src/vs/workbench/contrib/performance/browser/perfviewEditor.ts`, line 218

## Fix

Change the second `LifecyclePhase.Starting` to `LifecyclePhase.Ready`:

```typescript
`${(contribTimings.get(LifecyclePhase.Starting)?.length ?? 0) + (contribTimings.get(LifecyclePhase.Ready)?.length ?? 0)} blocking startup`
```
microsoft/vscode#304451.title: Performance view: blocking startup count uses LifecyclePhase.Starting twice instead of Starting + Ready
